sections.paragraph:
m. (f. Isa 7:20?) with suff. תַּעְרִי pr. making naked, emptying (for תַּעֲרֶה from the root עָרָה)-

(1) a razor (pr. a naked thin plate, or making the skin bare), Num 6:5 8:7 Isa 7:20. תַּעַר הַסֹּפֵר a writer’s pen-knife, with which he sharpens his reed, Jer 36:23.

(2) the sheath of a sword (perhaps so called from emptiness, see the root Piel, No. 3). 1Sa 17:51 Eze 21:8, 10 Eze 21:10, 35 Eze 21:35 Jer 47:6.
